foothold-customer.title: Foothold customer
foothold-customer.prompt: Who is the foothold? Overshot mainstream customer (low-end disruption) or non-consumer the incumbent never served (new-market disruption)? Be specific about segment, geography, occasion.
examples: African feature-phone users — non-consumers from Apple's perspective
Used-car buyers who refuse to negotiate at dealerships — overshot mainstream
Chinese users without easy specialist access — non-consumers of in-person healthcare

worse-on-dimension.title: Worse on dimension
worse-on-dimension.prompt: On which dimensions valued by mainstream customers is the disruptor *initially inferior*? Name the dimension and quantify the gap — Christensen requires this.
examples: Sound quality lower than CDs initially
Camera resolution lower than DSLRs in 2007
Specialist depth lower than in-person doctor visits

acceptable-on-dimension.title: Acceptable on dimension
acceptable-on-dimension.prompt: What is the disruptor better on, that the foothold customer values enough to accept the inferiority above? Price, simplicity, accessibility, convenience, or context fit?
examples: Instant access + no commute (Ping An Good Doctor)

incumbent-rational-ignore.title: Incumbent's rational reason to ignore
incumbent-rational-ignore.prompt: Why will incumbents rationally *not* respond? Typical: thin margin, small market today, cannibalisation risk against current high-margin products. Their customer interview correctly tells them to ignore you.
examples: Apple cannot serve <$50 phones without destroying its brand-margin model
Traditional dealerships cannot match Carvana's price without breaking franchise economics
Established hospitals cannot scale specialist time at telehealth unit price

upmarket-migration-path.title: Upmarket migration path
upmarket-migration-path.prompt: Concrete trajectory: how will you improve along the mainstream dimensions while preserving foothold advantage? Product milestones, customer-segment migration, scale economics.
examples: Feature phone → Android smartphone for emerging-market mainstream
Trade-in → certified pre-owned → near-new inventory expansion
Generalist tele-consult → specialist tele-consult → partnered in-person care

riskiest-assumptions.title: Riskiest assumptions
riskiest-assumptions.prompt: Which assumption will break the case if false? Pick from: foothold customer willingness, improvement trajectory feasibility, incumbent inattention duration, organisational insulation from parent's margin metrics.
examples: ≥X% of foothold customers will actually pay the price we promised
We can improve mainstream dimension Y by Z% in 24 months
Incumbent will not respond within 36 months because of cannibalisation risk
